name: Carson Smith
abstract: Carson Raymond Smith (January 9, 1931, San Francisco – November 2, 1997, Las Vegas) was an American jazz double-bassist. Carson is the older brother of jazz musician and composer Putter Smith.

concerts.label: Billie Holiday: Lady Sings the Blues

works.t: Please Don't Talk About Me When I'm Gone

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Billie Holiday: Lady Sings the Blues

works.t: Right This Way: I'll Be Seeing You
What a Little Moonlight Can Do
Please Don't Talk About Me When I'm Gone

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: I contacted Carson Smith who would end up, you know, with Gerry and I... So the original quintet was going to be Jim Hall on guitar, your drums, Carson Smith on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Carson Smith was the bass player, and Chico and me, I guess that’s it. The quintet. So it was a perfect job for me and we played at this club in Long Beach
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Carson Smith, Paul Horn, John Pisano and Fred Katz. ... The group was intact with Buddy and Jim Hall, and Carson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 30, 2000
